objective: Find the write-to-execute bridge for privilege escalation. The command injection chain as syswatch is validated but plugins/ is root:root 755. We need to find how to get a .sh file into plugins/ or find an alternative root execution path from syswatch-writable locations (logs/, backup/).

current_access.privilege_level: user (uid 1001) + cmd injection as syswatch (uid 984) via Flask GUI
current_access.access_vector: SSH: ssh -i ../shared/raw/devarea_key dev_ryan@10.129.10.193. Command injection as syswatch via forged Flask session on localhost:7777.
current_access.notes: Forged Flask session cookie in exploit_log.md. Cmd injection payload: pipe + xxd hex decode. Regex blocks ;/\&.<>\r and uppercase.

in_scope: Read the COMPLETE live /opt/syswatch/syswatch.sh via command injection (as syswatch, who can read it)
Diff live syswatch.sh against zip version — look for NEW subcommands not in the zip
Check if syswatch.sh has backup, restore, plugin-add, plugin-install, or deploy subcommands
Check if sudo syswatch.sh plugin <name> uses the plugin name unquoted anywhere ($ expansion)
Check if any root-executed scripts (monitor.sh, log_monitor.sh) source or read from /opt/syswatch/logs/ or /opt/syswatch/backup/
Check if common.sh or syswatch.conf are writable or symlink-replaceable by syswatch
Check for symlink attack opportunities: can syswatch create symlinks in logs/ or backup/ that get followed by root processes?
Read /etc/syswatch.env for the full environment the services run with
out_of_scope: Do not attempt exploitation. Do not write files to plugins/. Do not modify existing scripts. Report findings and return.
allowed_actions: File reads via command injection as syswatch (cat, head, ls -la)
File reads via SSH as dev_ryan
Permission and ACL checks (stat, getfacl, ls -la)
Reading systemd units and timer configs
completion_criteria: Full live syswatch.sh read and analyzed for write-to-execute mechanisms
All root-executed scripts checked for sourcing from writable locations
Clear finding: either a bridge mechanism exists (report it) or no bridge exists (report exhaustion with evidence)
return_conditions: Bridge mechanism found — return immediately with details
Shell dies and cannot be re-established within 3 attempts
notes: CRITICAL CONTEXT: ELLIOT's truncated syswatch.sh output stopped at reload_web(). The full script is 6103 bytes and likely has additional subcommands after the web management functions. The setup.sh listed subcommands: web, web-stop, web-restart, web-status, plugin <name>, plugins, logs, --version, --help. But the LIVE script may have MORE subcommands added after the zip was created (zip is from Dec 14, script may have been updated). The $ in SAFE_PLUGIN_REGEX is interesting — test if sudo syswatch.sh plugin '$HOME' or similar causes unexpected behavior. Also check: does syswatch.sh 'plugin' subcommand run the plugin via 'bash $PLUGIN_DIR/$name' (unquoted) or 'bash "$PLUGIN_DIR/$name"' (quoted)? If unquoted, globbing or word splitting could be exploitable.
